name: Basic Holiday Butter Cookies-pt 1
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Grease two baking sheets with half a teaspoon of shortening each.
- Optionally, line the sheets with parchment paper; do not grease them.
3. In a large bowl, beat together butter and the remaining 1/4 cup shortening until even in color.
4. Add sugar and beat until fluffy, about 3-5 minutes.
5. In a small bowl, beat yolks, vanilla, and light cream together.
6. Slowly pour the yolk mixture into the butter mixture. Continue beating until thoroughly incorporated.
7. In a separate large bowl, sift flour, salt, cornstarch, and baking powder together.
8. Gently fold the flour mix into the butter mix in three additions, beating each addition until fully incorporated.
9. Scrape down the sides of the bowls occasionally.
10. Place four sheets of plastic wrap on a work surface.
11. Place about 1/4 of the dough on each sheet of plastic.
12. Form each clump of dough into a disk and wrap in plastic.
To Bake:
13. Cut, form, or shape one disk of dough into 24 cookies according to individual directions below.
14. Place the cookies on prepared sheets.
15. Bake for 15-20 minutes, depending on size and shape (see individual instructions).
16. Remove cookies from the oven and immediately transfer them to a rack.
17. Allow cookies to cool completely before storing in tins or freezing, wrapped in freezer wrap or plastic freezer bags.
18. Cookies can be frozen for 8-12 months.
